subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate policies: Climate policies are only for profit
subnarratives.evidence_quote: The C40 World Mayors Summit is more than just another chance to recognize the magnitude of this make-or-break decade for climate action – it’s a chance to elevate the solutions cities are deploying at a neighborhood level that have a real impact on people’s daily lives.
subnarratives.reasoning: The article frames the summit and Garcetti's participation as hypocritical and performative, implying the action is for show or political/bureaucratic gain rather than genuine environmental concern, especially when contrasted with the carbon cost of his travel and problems at home. The sarcastic headline and tone suggest the trip is about posturing, not effective action.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Climate movement is corrupt
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a “global coalition of mayors” at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
subnarratives.reasoning: The quote highlights hypocrisy by suggesting the mayor's actions (burning fossil fuels for travel) contradict the stated goals of the climate movement, implying the movement or its participants are not genuinely committed and are engaged in unethical or self-serving practices.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Controversy about green technologies: Renewable energy is unreliable
subnarratives.evidence_quote: A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote criticizes green transportation infrastructure projects (high-speed rail, passenger rail) by highlighting their failures and suspensions, directly supporting the subnarrative that renewable/green energy solutions are not dependable.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Controversy about green technologies: Renewable energy is costly
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Garcetti’s “Green New Deal” calls for shuttering natural gas plants; it has been opposed by public works unions, and lost the only serious electoral challenge it faced in a 2019 city council race.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames the Green New Deal (a plan for clean energy transition) as facing opposition and electoral defeat, implying it is politically and economically costly and inefficient to implement.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Controversy about green technologies: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: According to the ICAO Carbon Emissions Calculator, Garcetti’s trip would have burned 1275.6 kg of carbon if he flew economy class, and twice as much if he flew business or “premium” class (assuming a stop in Atlanta). That is one-fourth (economy) or one-half (premium) the global average per capita use of carbon per year. It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote criticizes the hypocrisy and practical failure of climate action (attending a summit via high-emission travel), which supports the parent narrative of controversy about green technologies but does not fit the specific definitions about renewable energy's danger, unreliability, cost, or nuclear energy.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement implicitly criticizes the necessity of the climate summit by questioning why it required physical travel, thereby suggesting the event and its goals are not serious or urgent enough to justify the associated carbon emissions. This downplays the importance of coordinated climate action.
